main_turf.bias_notes: Anti-clockwise provincial circuit. Sweeping turns with excellent cambering. Features a unique 1,000m straight chute, making it the only straight course in Western Australia. Inside barriers hold a mild advantage in 1200m sprint races.
main_turf.post_bias: Inside barriers (1-6) hold a slight statistical edge in 1200m sprint events off the turn, but the 1000m straight chute provides a fair test across all barriers.

scarpside_turf.bias_notes: The outer/longer circuit featuring the longest home straight in Western Australia (456m). Highly accommodating to swoopers and off-pace runners due to the expansive straightaway.
scarpside_turf.post_bias: Extremely fair course. Outside barriers are non-detrimental over 1300m to 1800m trips due to long backstretch runs.

handicapping_angles.straight_1000m_specialists: Upgrade horses with proven straight-track experience for 1000m events. Without turns, horses with sustained balance and true straight-line speed excel.
handicapping_angles.scarpside_swooper_upgrade: When racing on the Scarpside track layout, upgrade strong late closers ('S' or 'P' types) who benefit from the grueling 456m home stretch.
handicapping_angles.main_course_sprint_bias: On the original course, favor horses with tactical speed ('E' or 'EP') in 1200m races to secure position before the home turn.
